news.headline: Timios in PLANO, Texas adds Slate Mortgage Solutions team, launches new Capital Markets Division.
news.summary: Plano, Texas - Timios, Inc. (Timios), a prominent national company offering title and escrow services, has recently introduced its new Capital Markets Division. This expansion comes after successfully bringing on board the proficient team from Slate Mortgage Solutions. The development reflects Timios' commitment to enhancing its services and solidifying its position in the industry. This strategic move is aimed at capitalizing on new opportunities and furthering the company's growth.

news.headline: EZCORP reported Q1 results for FY 2025, with PLO up 13% to $274.8M, net income up 9% to $31.0M, and adjusted EBITDA up 12% to $53.0M. CEO positive on growth momentum.
news.summary: EZCORP, Inc. reported its Q1 results for the quarter ending December 31, 2024. The company's Pawn loans outstanding (PLO) increased by 13% to $274.8 million. Net income was up by 9% to $31.0 million, and on an adjusted basis, net income increased by 14% to $32.6 million. Adjusted EBITDA saw a 12% increase to $53.0 million. Total revenues were up by 7% to $320.2 million, with gross profit also rising by 7% to $185.4 million. The company's CEO highlighted a strong start to fiscal 2025, with record revenues and PLO driven by customer demand for cash solutions. In the U.S., PLO grew by 15%, and in Latin America, PLO rose by 19% on a constant currency basis. Net inventory increased by 21% due to higher PLO. The company remains focused on driving growth through strategic initiatives and expects to deliver another record year of performance in fiscal 2025. Cash and cash equivalents at the end of the quarter were reported at $174.5 million. The company will host a conference call on February 6, 2025, to discuss its Q1 fiscal results. EZCORP is a leading pawn transactions provider in the U.S. and Latin America.

news.headline: Gold price hits record high at $2,860/oz as central banks buy over 1,000 tons in Q4, setting a new 2024 record of 4,974 tons. Geopolitical tensions fuel demand.
news.summary: The price of gold, as monitored by the SPDR Gold Trust (NYSE:SPY), closed at a remarkable $2,860 per ounce on Wednesday, extending its record-breaking streak for the fifth consecutive winning session. This surge in value comes as central banks and investors flock to gold as a safe haven amidst rising geopolitical tensions and economic uncertainties. According to the World Gold Council's report released on Wednesday, total gold demand, inclusive of over-the-counter investment, saw a 1% year-over-year increase in the fourth quarter, setting a new high of 4,974 tons for 2024. Central banks led the surge by purchasing over 1,000 tons of gold for the third year in a row, with a significant spike in buying during the fourth quarter amounting to 333 tons. The World Gold Council emphasized that this trend is being driven by central banks and investors seeking refuge, propelling demand and value to unprecedented levels. Central banks' rush to buy gold remains a key question amid the ongoing market shifts.

news.headline: Catch latest earnings reports with numbers on Closing Bell show on Bloomberg.
PotlatchDeltic signs lithium/bromine lease with TETRA in Arkansas on 900 acres for brine exploration and production.
news.summary: In Spokane, Washington, PotlatchDeltic Corporation (Nasdaq: PCH) finalized a mineral lease deal with TETRA Brine Leaseco LLC. This agreement grants TETRA exclusive rights to explore and produce brine on about 900 acres in Lafayette County, Arkansas. The area specified in the lease is part of the lithium-rich Smackover Formation. The lease is set for an initial five-year period.

news.headline: Lantronix board member Philip Brace leaves to be CEO at Skyworks; effective Feb. 5, 2025.
news.summary: On February 5, 2025, Lantronix Inc., a leading provider of IoT solutions (NASDAQ: LTRX), revealed that Philip Brace will step down from their Board of Directors. Brace is set to assume the role of chief executive officer at Skyworks Solutions Inc. This change marks Brace's transition from Lantronix to Skyworks Solutions, commencing on the mentioned date.

news.summary: The latest data reveals that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to 4.8%, the lowest figure recorded in the past 10 years. This decrease represents approximately 300,000 more people becoming employed in the last quarter. Additionally, the economy has seen a growth of 3.5% in the same period, with over 500,000 new jobs created. The current inflation rate stands at 2.2%, showing a slight increase from the previous month. On a positive note, the average wage has also seen a rise of 2.6%, providing some relief to workers. These economic indicators point towards a stable and improving economic situation in the country.
